evidence_refs.quote: store foot traffic improved as mall visits recovered
per-order fulfillment costs fell on warehouse automation
rising crude oil prices lifted our freight and shipping costs
hourly store wages rose with minimum wage increases
warehouse hourly wages climbed amid tight labor markets

skips.why: Evidence quote ('delta marketplace take rate held steady') names a real cause — the delta marketplace take rate — but the coined name does not. 'unique' is a vague descriptor banned by R7, names no causal variable, and is absent from the source (fails R10's source-noun rule). No coined clean form (e.g. a take-rate name) exists in the catalog to rewrite to, and inventing one is forbidden, so skip.

Evidence ('gamma loyalty program enrollment expanded') names a valid brand-specific cause — gamma loyalty program enrollment — but the coined name 'gamma_unique' does not name it. 'unique' is a vague non-causal descriptor (R7) not found in the source (R10). No coined clean form (e.g. an enrollment name) exists in the catalog to rewrite to, so skip rather than invent a name.
